approach: e2e-first (the deliverable IS the test). Production change is a single additive testid; Red→Green driven by running the new Playwright specs against a clean dev server with API mocking until both pass.
ci_policy: 方針 B (CI integration out of scope; local chromium e2e PASS is the acceptance bar). playwright.config.ts / .github workflows unchanged.

production_changes.change: Added data-testid="history-pane-expand" to the History expand <button> (aria-label="Expand history panel").
production_changes.risk: none — purely additive DOM attribute, no props/render/runtime behavior change; verified by full unit suite (6700 pass).

new_files.purpose: API-route mock (page.route over /api/) to render the worktree detail split UI without DB/git/tmux session; sessionStorage-guarded localStorage isolation (clears commandmate:terminalSplits:* + layout keys once per test); unique worktree IDs e2e-split-a / e2e-split-b; ensureFilesActivityVisible helper.

AC-27 Scenario 1: with >=4 parallel PaneResizer instances (ActivityPane + History + 2 split gaps), dragging split-resizer-0 sets body cursor to 'col-resize' DURING drag and reverts (not 'col-resize') AFTER. chromium-only self-skip; 1920x1080 viewport.
AC-27 Scenario 2: split count persists per worktreeId (A=3, B=1→2), switching back restores each worktree's own config (A=3, B=2). Verifies localStorage worktreeId scoping end-to-end.

verification.e2e_mobile_safari: 2 skipped (self-skip works; webkit installed to confirm)

debugging_notes: Shared :3000 server runs a DIFFERENT worktree (MyCodeBranchDesk) → served stale chunks (ChunkLoadError). Verified against a dedicated NODE_ENV=development CM_PORT=3217 dev server from this worktree; user's :3000 left untouched.
Initial split-subtree crash: useSlashCommands reads data.groups → mock must return { groups: [] }; FileTreeView reads rootData.items → mock must return { items: [] }.
Persistence spec initially failed because page.addInitScript runs on EVERY navigation, wiping persisted config; fixed with a sessionStorage guard so the clear runs once per test.
